seo.title: New Mexico CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to New Mexico - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer CNA license to new mexico, new mexico CNA endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Nursing
board.mailing_address:
New Mexico Board of Nursing
6301 Indian School Road NE, Suite 710
Albuquerque, NM 87110

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in New Mexico.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the NMBON Online Licensing Portal (bon.envisiongo.com) and click 'Register Now' to establish a new user account.

steps.title: Log In and Select Application Type
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account and select the appropriate CNA application type, such as 'Initial Certification,' 'Endorsement,' or 'Renewal'.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all sections of the online application form accurately, providing personal, educational, and relevant employment information.

steps.title: Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Digitally upload all necessary supporting documentation, which may include proof of training completion, background check consent forms, or out-of-state license verifications.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee directly through the NMBON Online Licensing Portal using a valid credit or debit card.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Application
steps.description: Review your entire application for completeness and accuracy before final submission. Monitor the status of your application through your NMBON Online Licensing Portal account.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does New Mexico CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in New Mexico. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: No, fingerprints are generally not required for CNA endorsement in New Mexico.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in NM.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to New Mexico.

hero.title: Become a CNA in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) certification in New Mexico, submit your application and all required documentation through the NMBON Online Licensing Portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in New Mexico
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to New Mexico CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: From the NMBON Online Licensing Portal, click 'Register Now' to create a new user account. Follow the prompts to set up your username, password, and security questions. Once registered, log in to access the application options.
